title: [BUG] Type for CellHyperlinkValue is wrong, sometimes has nested CellRichTextValue
author: Zegnat

comments.author: ajuvonen
comments.body: By coincidence, our project ran into the same issue just now, when we noticed .text doesn't return the textual representation of a link in cases where the cell included rich text formatting. Instead, the above occurs. Would be grateful if there were a dependable way to access the textual content of the cell that we wouldn't need to double check.

comments.author: skypesky
ajuvonen
ajuvonen
comments.body: The issue may be related to users who are saving .xlsx files with Apple Numbers, but I haven't been able to verify this.

comments.author: Wissam-Yelhow
comments.body:
Same issue here, I'm having to use cell.text.richText[0].text to get my value, and TypeScript isn't happy with it.
Any news about this?

comments.author: ajuvonen
comments.body: @Wissam-Yelhow If I understand exceljs rich text correctly, the content of the cell may be split in multiple rich text formats, so until a fix exists, you should pull the values from all possible array items and join them, not just look at the text property in the first item. Not sure if there's any shortcut for this.

comments.author: Wissam-Yelhow
comments.body:
Thanks @ajuvonen , we're currently using:
    
`return (cellValue as Exceljs.CellRichTextValue).richText.map(richText => richText.text).join(' ');`

comments.author: Zegnat
Zegnat
comments.body:
I’ve now published [a full reproduction repo](https://github.com/Zegnat/ts-exceljs-issue-1988) that shows this problem. It also includes [an XLXS file that shows this behaviour](https://github.com/Zegnat/ts-exceljs-issue-1988/raw/main/example.xlsx), having both a field where `text` is a string and one where `text` is a CellRichTextValue.

Hope that makes it super clear :D

comments.author: skypesky
